phase3_findings.description: Suite FAILS at committed HEAD: #434 stale-prose self-scan flags 3 PR-authored lines (OVERVIEW:567 R4, phase-3-review:119 R4, review-and-fix:1054 R3); dirty-tree runs skipped the self-scan so prior 'pass' evidence did not cover clean HEAD
phase-3-review.md:117 names a fabricated consumer ('the weekly retrospective joins on defect_signature')
phase-3-review.md:119 absolute 'which no tier can reconstruct' contradicted by the same diff for cap_drops
usage_blocks over-counts usage:null carriers on a mixed transcript
encoding-pass jq failure unconditionally attributed to the file
marker-path pin greps comment copies, not code
matcher-probe.yml header omits the two #437 jobs
circular committed-evidence pointer in the OBSERVED usage row
empty-argument invocation untested (rating 3)
stop-hook with fully unrunnable jq untested (rating 3)
_(no object keys found)_ placeholder arm unexercised (rating 2, near-unreachable)

fix_decisions.claim_text: _(no object keys found)_ placeholder arm unexercised
fix_decisions.evidence: rating-2 completeness-only gap; the arm is near-unreachable (any parsed file with a result event has keys) and the sibling placeholder IS asserted — not worth a fixture

fix_decisions.claim_text: drop 'per-message' from the cloud-tier rows (parentage unproven)
fix_decisions.evidence: the prior formal review (run 29217200250) prescribed exactly 'per-message tokens' to align changeset/efficiency-trace wording, and the 'What it does NOT settle' section plus the reworded usage-row parenthetical already carry the attribution caveat; applied the narrower circular-pointer fix instead

fix_decisions.claim_text: prior iter records' verification_evidence 'pass' is false for committed HEAD
fix_decisions.evidence: verification_evidence records the command actually run that iteration (documented pre-3.5 caveat); those dirty-tree runs did pass — the gap was the #434 self-scan's dirty-tree skip, now fixed at HEAD and recorded here rather than by rewriting historical logs

fix_decisions.claim_text: stale-prose STALE rows adjudicated false-positive by the prior cloud review
fix_decisions.evidence: semantically false-positive per the prior review, but the #434 self-scan makes them a deterministic suite FAILURE at HEAD, so they were reworded (deny/permit token de-collision, count de-adjacency) with semantics preserved
shape 2+3: sole observable impact is stderr breadcrumb wording (emitted verdict stays a correct 'unavailable', exit 0); uncorroborated single-source from silent-failure-hunter, no Phase-2 FAIL

fix_decisions.claim_text: hook launch failure indistinguishable from did-not-fire
fix_decisions.evidence: shape 2+3: impact is diagnostic distinguishability of a repo-internal probe's negative evidence; docs already forbid over-reading 'did not fire'; single-source

shape 3: single-source pr-test-analyzer (empirical over-grader), self-rated 5/10 with the mitigation that the job is repo-internal informational; no current wrong output — coverage hardening, applied anyway

verification_evidence.command: lib/test/run.sh (clean tree, post-commit) + shellcheck + ruff + stale-prose-lint rc=0

stale_prose_check: post-fix branch diff at HEAD: rc=0; rows: 1 VERIFIED (phase-3-review:113 R4), 1 UNRESOLVABLE (review-and-fix:674 R3 'Step 3 item' regex artifact — informational)
devflow_reflection: Verification posture: 7 lite + 36 orchestrator-direct executed probes (incl. live falsification of the redaction boundary and completion gate); 0 verifier subagents dispatched — executed evidence preferred over code-reading verifiers on a suite-covered surface.

The final-pass reviewer's Critical was real and reproducible only on a clean tree: the #434 self-scan skips itself when the tree is dirty, so every in-loop dirty-tree suite run reported green while committed HEAD was red. Recorded here as the dirty-tree-skew caveat the reviewer recommended.

SKILL.md prose edits (review-and-fix:1054, phase-3-review 117/119) were made under the fix-loop's own RED->GREEN evidence (the #434 self-scan RED at HEAD -> reworded -> suite GREEN); the interactive writing-skills invocation was not dispatched (autonomous run; deviation recorded).

stale-prose 'Step 3 item 6' regex artifact: _COUNT_RE matches the ordinal collision; fixed by possessive rewording on the one diff-added STALE line only (line 674's identical artifact is UNRESOLVABLE and never gates).

shadow.checklist_note: 38 deduped shadow items (manual cross-batch dedup — deduper agent skipped, recorded deviation), all PASS via lite probes + orchestrator-direct executed probes incl. the 64/65 safekey boundary
shadow.transient_retry: final-pass reviewer returned mid-suite; single bounded re-contact (SendMessage) recovered its full report — budget spent

phase3_findings.description: stop-hook-probe.sh misattributes an unrunnable jq to the transcript/payload
settings.json hook launch failure indistinguishable from did-not-fire
hook-probe inline branch selector should be extracted to a suite-drivable scripts/ helper (repo convention, PR #367 precedent)
fallback transcript_path_present=false polarity, absent-arm max_usage_figure, empty-string transcript_path untested
'Nothing in the tree ingests execution_file prose' overbroad on a literal reading
changeset encoding enumeration omits the fourth value 'unavailable'
efficiency-trace.md two-sentence parenthetical (style only)
workflow-side marker pin is comment-satisfiable (bare literal grep)
'half the time' rebuttal lacks an antecedent
explicit permission_denials_count: null lands on unavailable via an undocumented, unpinned path

shadow.note: all three Important-candidates flagged by the over-grade gate (shapes 2/3) and severity-calibrated to Suggestion with recorded evaluations; iteration 2 applies them as trivial-and-clearly-correct Suggestion fixes per Step 3 item 2 and the user's fix-all directive
